An inhabited island floating in the Kitan Strait. Belongs to Wakayama City, Wakayama Prefecture.
With an area of 5.3 square kilometers, the population is approximately 700 people.
The main industries are tourism and fishing. Specialty products are mugwort mochi and salted squid guts.
It was once called Onogoro Island, but during the late Kyoho period it was renamed Hitogashima, and the current name Hitogashima first appeared in the Meiji era.
It is known for the Hiruko legend and the gunpowder fortress ruins built in the Meiji period.
Many residents commute to the mainland using the Hitogashima Ferry.
